lesson.title: Multiplying & Dividing Exponents
lesson.body: When working with polynomial exponents: **Product rule**: xᵃ · xᵇ = xᵃ⁺ᵇ. **Power rule**: (xᵃ)ᵇ = xᵃᵇ. **Power of a product**: (xy)ᵃ = xᵃyᵃ. **Quotient rule**: xᵃ/xᵇ = xᵃ⁻ᵇ (for x ≠ 0). **Power of a quotient**: (x/y)ᵃ = xᵃ/yᵃ. **Zero exponent**: x⁰ = 1 (x ≠ 0). **Negative exponent**: x⁻ᵃ = 1/xᵃ. These rules combine when simplifying complex expressions.

rooms.question: Simplify: (3x²y)(4xy³)
steps: x^2 \cdot x^1 = x^3, \quad y^1 \cdot y^3 = y^4
rooms.explanation: Multiply coefficients: 12. Apply product rule to each variable: x²·x = x³; y·y³ = y⁴. Result: 12x³y⁴. The 7x³y⁴ error adds coefficients (3+4=7) instead of multiplying.

rooms.question: Simplify: (2a³b²)³
rooms.explanation: Raise each factor to the power 3: 2³=8; (a³)³=a⁹; (b²)³=b⁶. Result: 8a⁹b⁶. The 2a⁹b⁶ error only raises variables to the power, forgetting the coefficient. The 6a⁹b⁶ error multiplies 2×3=6 instead of cubing (2³=8).

rooms.question: Simplify: 15x⁵y³ ÷ (3x²y)
rooms.explanation: Divide coefficients: 15/3=5. Apply quotient rule: x⁵/x²=x³; y³/y=y². Result: 5x³y². The 5x⁷y⁴ error adds exponents instead of subtracting them (uses product rule when it should use quotient rule).

rooms.question: Simplify: (x³/y²)⁴
rooms.explanation: Apply power rule to both numerator and denominator: (x³)⁴ = x¹² and (y²)⁴ = y⁸. Result: x¹²/y⁸. The x⁷/y⁶ error adds the outer exponent (3+4=7, 2+4=6) instead of multiplying.

rooms.question: Order these expressions from smallest to largest exponent on x (after simplifying):
rooms.explanation: Results: x⁴/x=x³, x²·x³=x⁵, (x²)³=x⁶, x⁸/x²=x⁶. Order by exponent: 3 < 5 < 6 = 6. Note two expressions both give x⁶ — either order is acceptable for those two.

boss.name: The Exponent Shade
boss.taunt: Add the powers. Multiply the pain.

questions.question: Simplify: (4x²y³)² / (2xy²)
questions.explanation: Numerator: (4x²y³)² = 16x⁴y⁶. Divide: 16/2=8; x⁴/x=x³; y⁶/y²=y⁴. Result: 8x³y⁴. The 16x³y⁴ error skips dividing the coefficient.

questions.question: Simplify and write with positive exponents: x⁻³ · x⁵ / x²
questions.explanation: Combine exponents: x^(−3+5−2) = x^0 = 1. Any nonzero base raised to the 0 power equals 1. The x⁴ error adds the numerator exponents and ignores the denominator: −3+5=2, forgetting −2.

questions.question: Simplify: (2x³)⁴ / (4x²)²
questions.explanation: Numerator: (2x³)⁴ = 2⁴x¹² = 16x¹². Denominator: (4x²)² = 16x⁴. Divide: 16x¹²/16x⁴ = x⁸. The 4x⁸ error divides 16/16 as 4 instead of 1.
